findings.details: Post 06 is titled '50 Questions to Ask Your Parents Before It's Too Late' but its primary_keyword is 'questions to ask dad before its too late'. The title is gender-neutral/parents-generic while the keyword is dad-specific. Either the title undersells the targeted keyword (and won't rank well for 'dad' queries) or the keyword is wrong for the article's actual scope.
findings.suggested_fix: Either retitle Post 06 to '50 Questions to Ask Your Dad Before It's Too Late' to match the dad-targeted keyword, or change primary_keyword to 'questions to ask parents before its too late' to match the parents-scoped title.
findings.title: Post 06 title says 'Parents' but primary_keyword targets 'dad'

findings.details: Post 06 has event_date=2026-06-21 (Father's Day) and window=SOON, implying it's a Father's Day push. But the title targets 'parents' generally and the keyword is the evergreen 'before it's too late' angle, not a Father's Day angle. This will cause it to be auto-archived after June 21 even though the content is evergreen, losing the post from the active view.
findings.suggested_fix: Move Post 06 to window=EVERGREEN with event_date=null so it doesn't get swept after Father's Day. If a Father's-Day-themed companion is wanted, create a separate post.
findings.title: Post 06 tagged to Father's Day event but content is generic parents

findings.details: Post 01 ('What to Ask Your Dad Before Father's Day: 50 Questions Worth Recording in His Own Voice', publish_by 2026-04-30) and Post 06 ('50 Questions to Ask Your Parents Before It's Too Late', publish_by 2026-05-31, event_date Father's Day) are both 50-question listicles tied to the same Father's Day event. Risk of keyword cannibalization between the two on Listn's own site.
findings.suggested_fix: Differentiate angles explicitly: keep Post 01 as the Father's Day-targeted dad-specific listicle, and re-scope Post 06 to a different format (e.g., 'How to Get Your Parent to Actually Open Up' or 'What to Do With the Recording After') so they don't compete for the same SERP intent.
findings.title: Posts 01 and 06 are near-duplicates ('50 Questions' for Father's Day)
